title: Chokepoints + Schedule Readiness
scope: Static planning/readiness reference for chokepoint exposure, carrier cutoff logic, free-time risk, and missed-service-cycle impact. Not a live routing/schedule engine.
design_principle: Code does the math; LLM does judgment. SEA-I provides deterministic rules and confidence caps for readiness reasoning.

sources.title: UNCTAD Review of Maritime Transport 2024
sources.use: Strategic chokepoint risk context for Suez, Panama, Red Sea and global maritime disruption exposure.

sources.title: Panama Canal Authority - Advisories to Shipping
sources.use: Official source for maximum authorized draft, booking slot, and transit advisory changes.

sources.title: Panama Canal Authority - Increase in draft and daily transit announcements
sources.use: Example that Panama Canal transit slots and draft limits change based on Gatun Lake levels.

sources.title: Suez Canal Authority official site
sources.use: Official source for tolls, navigation circulars, toll calculator and toll tables.

sources.title: Suez Canal Authority - Navigation Circulars
sources.use: Official source for current navigation circulars and toll/rebate updates.

sources.title: Maersk support - vessel cut-off times
sources.use: Carrier example showing cutoffs include container gate-in, VGM, and shipping instruction deadlines.

sources.title: Maersk support - detention and demurrage
sources.use: Carrier explanation of demurrage, detention, and free-time concepts.

sources.title: Maersk schedules
sources.use: Example of carrier schedule lookup source; not ingested as static schedule data.

known_limitations: Does not include live canal status, closure notices, or current draft/transit restrictions.
Does not include live carrier schedules or exact sailing-specific cutoff timestamps.
Does not include live demurrage/detention tariffs by carrier/country/terminal.
Does not include live security advisories, war-risk premiums, insurance status, or AIS tracking.
Chokepoint exposure is a planning factor unless a live advisory makes it a hard gate.
Weekly missed-sailing impact is an authored planning heuristic and must be replaced by actual service frequency when available.

confidence_policy.verified: Directly supported by official authority/carrier/intergovernmental source.
confidence_policy.verified_reference_live_status_required: The concept/source is verified, but current status must be checked live.
confidence_policy.authored: Cortex-authored operational logic derived from verified concepts.
confidence_policy.estimated: Planning approximation only; not a booking guarantee.
